steps.text: Cook the rice: In a saucepan, bring chicken stock to a boil. Add rinsed rice, reduce heat to low, cover, and cook until tender and liquid is absorbed. Remove from heat and keep covered.
Brown the meats: Heat oil and butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add ground pork and cook until browned, breaking it up finely.
Add chicken livers: Stir in chopped chicken livers. Cook, stirring frequently, until they break down and coat the meat mixture, forming the characteristic dark color.
Saute the trinity: Add onion, bell pepper, and celery. Cook until very soft and aromatic. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.
Season: Stir in bay leaves, Cajun seasoning, thyme, paprika, black pepper, salt, Worcestershire sauce, and optional hot sauce. Cook 1–2 minutes to bloom spices.
Combine with rice: Add cooked rice to the skillet. Fold gently until evenly coated and heated through. Adjust seasoning as needed.
Finish and serve: Remove bay leaves. Garnish with green onions and parsley. Serve hot as a main dish or side.
